key_finding: K > V CROSS-MODEL REPLICATED at all positions. Qwen: early +66.7pp, mid +76.9pp, late +51.3pp. V-only=100% at early/mid (39/39). UNEXPECTED: Qwen K-direction is MORE robust than Llama (K-early 33.3% vs 0%). K/V norm ratio 2.2x (vs Llama 6.4x) — K>V persists regardless. Digital fragility is magnitude-specific, not direction-specific.
evidence_impact: K > V universality confirmed across 2 model families x 3 positions = 6 independent conditions. V-only immunity at early/mid: 178/178 combined. K/V norm ratio differs 3x between models but qualitative finding is identical — functional, not energy-based. New finding: perturbation TYPE matters for digital/analog encoding distinction.
next_planned: Priority: (1) Third model family (Mistral/Phi) for K-V. (2) Qwen-Instruct position x K-V to separate Base/Instruct from model-family effect. (3) K-only PGD on Qwen to connect null-space and K-V findings. (4) Position x K-V with magnitude perturbation. (5) Consolidate findings into coherent narrative.

positional_dissociation.status: STRONG: universal across models. Late positions carry answer info via K-routing

geometric_dissociation.status: K-V dissociation CROSS-MODEL REPLICATED at ALL positions. Qwen gap +51-77pp, Llama +66-100pp. K/V norm ratio 2.2x (Qwen) vs 6.4x (Llama) — functional not energy

natural_usage.status: H2O/TC protection via position. R-KV shows removing redundant tokens IMPROVES reasoning

cross_model_replication.status: COMPLETE: K>V at 2 models x 3 positions. V immunity 178/178. Dose-response on 2 families. Need 3rd family

encoding_strategy.status: REFINED: Digital fragility is magnitude-specific (Qwen cliffs). Direction perturbation: Qwen MORE robust. Perturbation type matters for encoding distinction

superadditive_interaction.status: Llama-specific (7.9-16.8x). Weak on Qwen (1.1-2.0x). Explained by staggered vs overlapping degradation windows

literature_convergence.status: 5+ independent groups converge. K-V dissociation is novel contribution beyond existing literature
